page: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC Milan processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz, the processor is configured with 1.0 thread per core, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25% of tested servers. Large-block memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, reaching 184.1 GB/sec for read operations and 88.68 GB/sec for write operations. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. The server demonstrates strong capabilities in bzip3 decompression and text processing, while database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput remain average.

Given its dedicated 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and lack of hyperthreading, the c3-32 offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads requiring predictable, non-shared CPU execution without the overhead of larger memory footprints. The bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD provides fast local storage, making it suitable for build systems, text processing, and single-threaded or moderately threaded compute tasks. It is well-suited for CI/CD pipelines, web serving, and lightweight database hosting where dedicated CPU resources are prioritized over massive memory capacity.
description: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC Milan processor with x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.3 GHz with hyperthreading disabled, it delivers 32.0 GB of RAM, translating to 2.0 GB per core. The server includes a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core CPU execution and large-block memory bandwidth, while multi-core operations and database tasks perform in the average tier. This hardware configuration provides a cost-effective solution for compute-bound workloads that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios. It is highly suited for software compilation, text processing, and dedicated web serving.
og_description: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized server with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for dedicated workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 32 GB RAM, and a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD.
tagline: A dedicated 16-core AMD EPYC compute instance with bundled NVMe storage, optimized for predictable single-threaded performance and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with hyperthreading disabled
32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
Bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage
Strong single-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth performance
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Best suited for software compilation, text processing, and web serving
